gaps.reason: stop-gate.sh must be marked executable (chmod +x). Git mode is 100644 (not 100755), confirming the executable bit is NOT set. This will cause the hook to fail on Unix. Agent 2 called this SKIP due to Windows platform, but the git object mode is platform-independent evidence of a missing executable bit.

certification_notes: Cross-check of Agent 2's verification against raw artifacts (stdout.txt, junit.xml, direct file inspection). All 21 passing tests confirmed in JUnit XML. All file existence claims confirmed via direct filesystem check. One gap identified: SPEC-011 requires hooks/stop-gate.sh to be marked executable (chmod +x), but git object mode is 100644 (not 100755), proving the executable bit was never set. This is platform-independent evidence from the git index and will cause hook invocation to fail on Unix. Agent 2 marked this SKIP citing Windows platform limitation, but git mode is authoritative regardless of platform. SPEC-018 (bash -n check for install.sh) remains an acceptable SKIP — bash is genuinely unavailable on Windows and cannot be evaluated without a Unix runner. 10 skipped bash-only tests in test_stop_gate_logic.TestStopGateSh are correctly documented as Unix-only. certification=false due to 1 unresolved gap (SPEC-011).
